```crystal
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  return 0_i64 if n == 1

  # Precompute primes up to the maximum number in nums using Sieve of Eratosthenes
  max_num = nums.max || 1
  is_prime = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.isqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i).step(to: max_num, by: i) { |j| is_prime[j] = false }
    end
  end

  # Build a map from primes to indices where nums[j] is divisible by the prime
  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    next unless is_prime[num]
    prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
  end

  # BFS setup
  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = Deque.new([0_i64])
  visited[0] = true
  steps = 0_i64

  while !queue.empty?
    size = queue.size
    size.times do
      current = queue.shift
      return steps if current == n - 1

      # Check adjacent moves
      [current - 1, current + 1].each do |neighbor|
        if 0 <= neighbor < n && !visited[neighbor]
          visited[neighbor] = true
          queue << neighbor
        end
      end

      # Check prime teleportation
      num = nums[current]
      if is_prime[num]
        prime_to_indices[num].each do |j|
          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << j
          end
        end
        # Clear to avoid revisiting the same prime again
        prime_to_indices[num].clear
      end
    end
    steps += 1
  end

  -1_i64
end
```

```crystal
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  return 0_i64 if n == 1

  # Precompute primes up to the maximum number in nums using Sieve of Eratosthenes
  max_num = nums.max || 1
  sieve = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  sieve[0] = sieve[1] = false
  (2..Math.isqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
    if sieve[i]
      (i*i).step(max_num, i) { |j| sieve[j] = false }
    end
  end

  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    next unless sieve[num] # num is prime
    prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
  end

  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = Deque.new([0_i64])
  visited[0] = true
  steps = 0_i64

      return steps if current == n - 1

          queue << neighbor

          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << j
          end
        end
        # Clear to avoid reprocessing the same prime
        prime_to_indices[num].clear
      end
    end
    steps += 1
  end

end
```

```crystal
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  return 0_i64 if n == 1

  # Precompute primes up to the maximum number in nums using Sieve of Eratosthenes
  max_num = nums.max || 1
  is_prime = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_num).to_i).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) { |j| is_prime[j] = false }
    end
  end

  # Build a map from primes to indices where nums[j] is divisible by the prime
  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    if is_prime[num]
      prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
    else
      # For composite numbers, find all prime divisors
      temp = num
      (2..Math.sqrt(temp).to_i).each do |p|
        if is_prime[p] && temp % p == 0
          prime_to_indices[p.to_i64] << i.to_i64
          while temp % p == 0
            temp //= p
          end
        end
      end
      if temp > 1 && is_prime[temp]
        prime_to_indices[temp.to_i64] << i.to_i64
      end
    end
  end

  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = Deque.new([0_i64])
  visited[0] = true
  steps = 0_i64

  while !queue.empty?
    size = queue.size
    size.times do
      current = queue.shift
      return steps if current == n - 1

      # Move to adjacent indices
      [current - 1, current + 1].each do |neighbor|
        if 0 <= neighbor < n && !visited[neighbor]
          visited[neighbor] = true
          queue << neighbor
        end
      end

      # Check if current index has a prime number
      num = nums[current]
      if is_prime[num]
        prime = num.to_i64
        prime_to_indices[prime].each do |j|
          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << j
          end
        end
      end
    end
    steps += 1
  end

end
```

```crystal
  max_num = nums.max? || 0
  is_prime = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = false
  is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_num).to_i).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) { |j| is_prime[j] = false }
    end
  end

  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }

    if is_prime[num]
      prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
    else

      # But to optimize, we can factorize num and collect primes
      temp = num
      (2..Math.sqrt(temp).to_i).each do |p|
        if is_prime[p] && temp % p == 0
```

```crystal
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
```

```crystal
  max_num = nums.max
  is_prime = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = false
  is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_num).to_i).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) do |j|
        is_prime[j] = false
      end
    end
  end

  # Build a map from primes to indices where nums[j] is divisible by the prime
  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    if is_prime[num]
      prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
    else
      # For non-prime numbers, find all prime divisors
      temp = num
      (2..Math.sqrt(temp).to_i).each do |p|
        if is_prime[p] && temp % p == 0
          prime_to_indices[p.to_i64] << i.to_i64
          while temp % p == 0
            temp //= p
          end
        end
      end
      if temp > 1 && is_prime[temp]
        prime_to_indices[temp.to_i64] << i.to_i64
      end
    end
  end

  # BFS setup
  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = Deque.new([0_i64])
  visited[0] = true
  steps = 0_i64

  while !queue.empty?
    size = queue.size
    size.times do
      current = queue.shift
      return steps if current == n - 1

      # Check adjacent moves

        if 0 <= neighbor < n && !visited[neighbor]
          visited[neighbor] = true
          queue << neighbor
        end

      num = nums[current]
      if is_prime[num]
        prime_to_indices[num].each do |j|
          if !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << j
          end
        end
        # Clear to avoid revisiting
        prime_to_indices[num].clear
      else
        # Check all prime divisors of num
        temp = num
        (2..Math.sqrt(temp).to_i).each do |p|
          if is_prime[p] && temp % p == 0
            prime_to_indices[p.to_i64].each do |j|
              if !visited[j]

              end

            while temp % p == 0

            if !visited[j]

              queue << j
            end
          end
          prime_to_indices[temp.to_i64].clear
        end
      end
    end
    steps += 1
  end

end
```

```crystal
  max_num = nums.max || 1

  sieve[0] = sieve[1] = false
```
```crystal
  (2..Math.isqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
```
```crystal
    if sieve[i]

  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
```

```crystal
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  return 0_i64 if n == 1

  # Precompute primes up to the maximum number in nums using Sieve of Eratosthenes
  max_num = nums.max || 0
  is_prime = Array.new(max_num + 1, true)
  is_prime[0] = false
  is_prime[1] = false
  (2..Math.isqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
    if is_prime[i]
      (i*i).step(to: max_num, by: i) { |j| is_prime[j] = false }
    end
  end

  # Build a map from primes to indices where nums[j] is divisible by the prime
  prime_to_indices = Hash(Int64, Array(Int64)).new { |h, k| h[k] = [] of Int64 }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    if num >= 2 && is_prime[num]
      prime_to_indices[num] << i.to_i64
    end
  end

  # BFS setup
  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = Deque.new([0_i64])
  visited[0] = true
  steps = 0_i64

  while !queue.empty?
    level_size = queue.size
    level_size.times do
      current = queue.shift
      return steps if current == n - 1

      # Check adjacent moves
      [current - 1, current + 1].each do |neighbor|
        if 0 <= neighbor < n && !visited[neighbor]
          visited[neighbor] = true
          queue << neighbor
        end
      end

      # Check prime teleportation
      current_num = nums[current]
      if current_num >= 2 && is_prime[current_num]
        prime_to_indices[current_num].each do |j|
          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << j
          end
        end
        # Clear to avoid revisiting the same prime again
        prime_to_indices[current_num].clear
      end
    end
    steps += 1
  end

  -1_i64
end
```
